Who We Are

Built on Precision,
Driven by Excellence

Mahavir Industries has been a trusted name in precision metal fabrication since 2002. Located at XL-42, MIDC Ambad, Nashik — one of Maharashtra's premier industrial zones — we deliver high-quality engineered components to clients across India.

Our integrated facility houses CNC machining, laser cutting, shot blasting, hot-dip galvanizing, and powder coating under one roof — enabling complete production cycles with minimal lead times.

CNC MachiningLaser Cutting Sheet MetalShot Blasting GalvanizingPowder Coating OEM Production

What We Do

Our Capabilities

Full-cycle precision manufacturing — raw material to finished, coated, inspected component.

CNC Machining
High-accuracy CNC turning and milling for complex industrial components with tight tolerances and repeatable batch quality.
Laser Cutting
Precision laser cutting in mild steel, stainless, and aluminum — supporting profiles up to 25mm thickness.
Sheet Metal Fabrication
Shearing, bending, punching and forming for structural brackets, enclosures, and industrial frame applications.
Shot Blasting
Industrial surface preparation — removes rust, mill scale, and contaminants to ensure maximum zinc and coating adhesion.
Hot-Dip Galvanizing
In-house kettle: 3m × 1m × 1m. Full cathodic protection at 450°C. 45–85μm zinc coating. Up to 50+ year protection.
OEM / Custom Components
Drawing-based and sample-based OEM production with CAD support, strict tolerance adherence, and batch inspection.

DEDICATED SERVICE

Hot-Dip
Galvanising
Services

Industrial-grade hot-dip galvanizing with full in-house shot blasting — delivering superior corrosion resistance and zinc adhesion for structural and precision components.

Galvanizing Kettle Specifications
3
meters
LENGTH
1
meter
WIDTH
1
meter
DEPTH
Accommodates structural components, fabricated assemblies, and sheet metal parts up to 3 meters in length — one of the larger in-house kettles in the Nashik region.
Zinc coating: 45–85 μm average thickness
Pre-treatment: Shot blasting + flux treatment in-house
Suitable for structural steel, brackets, frames, poles
The Process

Step-by-Step Galvanizing

1
Degreasing & Cleaning
Components cleaned in alkaline solution to remove oils, grease, and organic contaminants from steel surface.
2
Shot Blasting
High-velocity steel shot removes mill scale, rust, and oxides, creating a clean anchor profile for superior zinc adhesion.
3
Pickling (Acid Treatment)
Dilute hydrochloric acid removes remaining oxides and activates the steel surface for optimum flux treatment.
4
Flux Treatment
Zinc ammonium chloride flux prevents re-oxidation and improves zinc wetting during molten zinc immersion.
5
Hot-Dip Galvanizing @ 450°C
Immersed in molten zinc — zinc reacts metallurgically with steel forming bonded zinc-iron alloy layers with full coverage.
6
Quality Inspection
Coating thickness measured with magnetic gauge. Visual inspection for uniform coverage before dispatch.
Pre-Treatment
Shot Blasting
Before Galvanizing

Shot blasting before galvanizing removes surface contaminants like rust, mill scale, and old paint — ensuring a chemically clean surface for superior zinc adhesion. It creates a roughened profile that enhances mechanical bonding and ensures uniform coating throughout the component.

Removes Mill Scale & Rust
Eliminates all surface oxides and contaminants for chemically pure base metal.
Creates Anchor Profile
Roughened surface increases mechanical bonding area for stronger zinc adhesion.
Ensures Uniform Coating
Consistent profile results in even zinc thickness across the entire component.
Process Flow
1
Raw steel component received
↓
2
Shot Blasting — surface prep
↓
3
Flux treatment applied
↓
4
Hot-dip galvanizing @ 450°C
↓
✓
Quality check & dispatch
Key Benefits

Why Galvanize?

50+ Year Corrosion Protection
Zinc coating provides 50+ years in rural, 25+ years in industrial environments — far exceeding paint systems.
Metallurgical Bond Strength
Zinc-iron alloy layers are harder than parent steel — cannot be peeled, chipped, or delaminated.
Zero Long-Term Maintenance
No re-painting or maintenance required for decades — dramatically reducing lifecycle costs.
Complete Surface Coverage
Penetrates internal surfaces, weld zones, recesses — areas inaccessible to brush or spray coatings.
Cathodic Protection
Zinc sacrifices itself to protect adjacent steel — even damaged areas remain protected by surrounding zinc.
Cost-Effective Long Term
Upfront galvanizing cost offset by eliminated maintenance, extended service life, and reduced replacement.

Why Choose Us

The Mahavir Galvanizing Edge

3m × 1m × 1m Kettle Capacity
Larger in-house galvanizing kettle in Nashik — large structural assemblies processed in single immersion batches.
Full In-House Shot Blasting
Complete pre-treatment in-house — no sub-contracting. Perfect surface prep every batch, every time.
22+ Years of Experience
Deep expertise in steel chemistry, coating requirements, and quality parameters developed over two decades.
Post-Galvanizing Powder Coating
Duplex system available — galvanize + powder coat for enhanced aesthetics and additional protection.
Quick Turnaround
Integrated facility means faster cycles — shot blasting and galvanizing coordinated without external sub-contractors.
MIDC Ambad, Nashik Location
Strategic industrial zone location with excellent logistics connectivity for clients across Maharashtra.
